questions.text: Which Solidity data type is best suited for tracking how many tokens each address holds?
questions.explanation: A mapping provides O(1) constant-time lookup and is the standard for balance tracking. Arrays would require loops and gas-heavy searches.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

A mapping provides O(1) constant-time lookup and is the standard for balance tracking. Arrays would require loops and gas-heavy searches.

questions.text: What is the default value of a uint256 state variable that has never been written to?
questions.explanation: All value types in Solidity have a zero-value default: uint is 0, bool is false, address is 0x0. Mappings return the default for any unset key.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

All value types in Solidity have a zero-value default: uint is 0, bool is false, address is 0x0. Mappings return the default for any unset key.

questions.text: Why is it recommended to use calldata instead of memory for read-only array parameters in external functions?

options.text: It avoids copying the entire array from calldata to memory, saving gas.
It allows the array to be modified within the function.
It makes the array persistent across transactions.
It automatically validates the array contents.

questions.explanation: memory triggers a copy from calldata; calldata reads directly from the transaction input. The gas savings are significant for large arrays.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

memory triggers a copy from calldata; calldata reads directly from the transaction input. The gas savings are significant for large arrays.

questions.text: Which statement about Solidity mappings is TRUE?

options.text: Mappings are stored in storage and cannot be iterated over.
Mappings can be stored in memory for temporary calculations.
Mappings automatically track which keys have been set.
Mappings can be iterated using a for loop.

questions.explanation: Mappings are storage-only and have no length or iteration capability. Use an auxiliary array if you need to enumerate keys.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

Mappings are storage-only and have no length or iteration capability. Use an auxiliary array if you need to enumerate keys.

questions.text: How does the EVM store multiple uint128 variables declared consecutively in a contract?

options.text: It packs them into a single 32-byte storage slot.
Each uint128 occupies its own 32-byte slot.
They are stored in memory, not storage.
Only one uint128 can exist per contract.

questions.explanation: The Solidity compiler packs consecutive small value types into single storage slots. Two uint128 values fit perfectly into one 32-byte slot, reducing storage costs.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The Solidity compiler packs consecutive small value types into single storage slots. Two uint128 values fit perfectly into one 32-byte slot, reducing storage costs.
